items.title: Waarom liet Kompany talentvolle Wanner naar PSV gaan?
items.summary: Paul Wanner maakte afgelopen zomer de overstap van Bayern München naar PSV. Bayern-trainer Vincent Kompany legt uit waarom hij de talentvolle middenvelder naar de Eredivisie heeft laten gaan.

items.title: Champions League news: Rosenior insists ‘incredible player’ Palmer is happy at Chelsea
items.summary: The head coach dismisses rumours about Palmer wanting to return to Manchester, saying playmaker ‘loves being here’ Continue reading...

items.title: Saudi Pro League lining up ambitious move for Ballon d'Or winner Dembele
items.summary: The Saudi Pro League is laying the groundwork for an ambitious summer move for Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembele.

items.title: Mooie reünie voor oud-NEC'er Larsson in Camp Nou: 'Ik heb een taak te doen'
items.summary: Het voetbal schrijft soms wel bijzonder mooie verhalen. Neem bijvoorbeeld de koppeling van Barcelona aan FC Kopenhagen in de Champions League. Woensdagavond betekent dat een Camp Nou-rentree voor het zoontje van een legendarische oud-speler van de Catalanen: Jordan Larsson.

items.title: 'PSG opent gesprekken met Luis Enrique, verrassende trainerskeuze Frankfurt'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal had 'beautiful' meeting after loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed he called his players for a meeting to "bring down the temperature" in the wake of their defeat to Manchester United.

items.title: 7 Greek fans die in crash en route to UEL game
items.summary: Seven fans of Greek soccer club PAOK Salonika died in a minibus crash in Romania while traveling to a Europa League game at Lyon, Greece's government confirmed Tuesday.

items.title: Abraham joins Villa 1 day after Besiktas transfer
items.summary: Tammy Abraham has signed for Aston Villa from Besiktas just one day after he joined the Turkish club a permanent transfer from Roma.

items.title: Rosenior squashes Palmer to Man United reports
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior said there is no point in discussing "unrealistic" reports Cole Palmer wants to join Manchester United.

items.title: What it's really like for a player switching clubs in the January transfer window
items.summary: ESPN FC analyst and former Man City defender Nedum Onuoha reveals how the days before the deadline can be full of uncertainty, selfishness and betrayal.

items.title: McTominay verwacht kolkend stadion en is blij om opleving Man Utd
items.summary: Buitengewoon belangrijke wedstrijd voor Napoli, woensdagavond in eigen stadion. Tegen Chelsea volstaat alleen een overwinning om min of meer zeker te zijn van een vervolg in de Champions League. Scott McTominay blikt vooruit.

items.title: Birmingham City presenteert voormalig Ajax-target Carlos Vicente
items.summary: Birmingham City heeft zich definitief verzekerd van de dienst van Carlos Vicente. Maandag werd al duidelijk dat de Spaanse vleugelaanvaller niet voor Ajax, maar voor het Championship had gekozen.

items.title: Spurs striker Kolo Muani 'fine' after car accident
items.summary: Randal Kolo Muani is "fine" after a "minor" car accident and should be available for Spurs' Champions League match at Eintracht Frankfurt, says Thomas Frank.

items.title: Premier League has the power but still faces reckoning with European giants | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: English clubs stroll through Champions League group phase but fatigue tends to take its toll by the spring Has there been a great game in the Champions League group stage this season? Probably not. Even if there had been, it almost certainly didn’t mean all that much. But that’s the way of the modern game: an extremely protracted clearing of the throat before the real business begins. Uefa will proudly tell the world that only six teams have nothing to play for in the final round of games on Wednesday, but whether it was worth 126 games to get to the mild peril of Napoli or Club Brugge possibly going out, or the questionable thrill of finding out whether Tottenham or Atalanta will have to endure the playoff round, is debatable. Continue reading...

items.title: Conte reunion a sharp reminder of Chelsea's fall
items.summary: Chelsea have not won the Premier League since Antonio Conte led them to the title in 2017 - and now they face the Italian's Napoli side in the Champions League.

items.title: Villa sign striker Abraham for £18.25m
items.summary: England striker Tammy Abraham completes an £18.25m return to Aston Villa from Turkish side Besiktas.

items.title: Spurs forward Kolo Muani ‘fine’ after 'minor' car accident
items.summary: Tottenham forward Randal Kolo Muani was involved in a “minor” car accident on Tuesday, but is "fine", according to head coach Thomas Frank and is expected to feature in Wednesday's Champions League clash at Eintracht Frankfurt.

items.title: Arne Slot says PSG European exit helped Liverpool win Premier League
items.summary: Manager wants players ‘to improve in both boxes’ Liverpool without Konaté and Gomez against Qarabag Arne Slot has said an early Champions League exit may have contributed to Liverpool winning the Premier League title last year but said his team must improve in both boxes to secure silverware this season. Slot has faced renewed criticism after his side’s 13-game unbeaten run ended at Bournemouth on Saturday, leaving the reigning champions sixth in the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: Cardiff, Lincoln ease to victory & Huddersfield score 50-YARD winner - EFL highlights
'The first person I called' - Koepka reveals Tiger role in PGA Tour return
items.summary: Brooks Koepka revealed the first person he called when he was released from his LIV contract was Tiger Woods, to discuss his return to the PGA Tour.
